description: Hand-written JSON Schema for [workspace.lints] in aipm.toml (Phase 1 — G1)
steps: Create schemas/ directory at the repository root
Create schemas/aipm.toml.schema.json using JSON Schema Draft 4 ($schema: http://json-schema.org/draft-04/schema#)
Set root type to object with additionalProperties: true (hides package/workspace/dependencies sections)
Define workspace.lints as nested object under properties.workspace.properties.lints
Add ignore.paths as an array of strings under workspace.lints.properties.ignore with additionalProperties: false

Add patternProperties regex matching all 19 rule IDs (skill/missing-name, skill/missing-description, skill/oversized, skill/name-too-long, skill/name-invalid-chars, skill/description-too-long, skill/invalid-shell, plugin/broken-paths, agent/missing-tools, hook/unknown-event, hook/legacy-event-name, source/misplaced-features, marketplace/source-resolve, marketplace/plugin-field-mismatch, plugin/missing-registration, plugin/missing-manifest, plugin/required-fields)
Define oneOf for rule values: string enum [allow, warn, warning, error, deny] OR object with level (required string enum) and ignore (optional array of strings)
Set additionalProperties: false on lints object to catch typos in rule IDs
Add x-taplo annotations with initKeys: [workspace.lints] for auto-generation hints
Add description fields for all properties to enable hover documentation in Taplo
Validate the schema by testing against existing fixture aipm.toml files (fixtures/standalone-plugin/aipm.toml, fixtures/workspace-no-deps/aipm.toml)
Verify a mistyped rule ID (e.g., skill/mising-name) produces a validation error
Verify valid [workspace.lints] config passes schema validation
Verify non-lints sections (package, dependencies) are allowed without validation errors (additionalProperties: true)

description: SchemaStore.org submission for zero-install schema discovery (Phase 1 — G2)
steps: Fork the SchemaStore/schemastore repository on GitHub
Copy schemas/aipm.toml.schema.json to src/schemas/json/aipm.toml.json in the SchemaStore fork
Add catalog entry to src/api/json/catalog.json: { name: aipm.toml, description: AI Package Manager configuration, fileMatch: [aipm.toml], url: https://json.schemastore.org/aipm.toml.json }
Create positive test file at src/test/aipm.toml/valid.toml with a valid [workspace.lints] section containing rule overrides
Create negative test file at src/test/aipm.toml/invalid.toml with an unknown rule ID to verify schema rejects it
Run SchemaStore's test suite to verify the schema passes
Submit PR to SchemaStore/schemastore with the schema, catalog entry, and test files
Verify that after merge, Taplo auto-discovers the schema for any file named aipm.toml

description: Add field_value_range() shared helper for frontmatter range computation (Phase 2 — G4 prerequisite)
steps: Add a pub fn field_value_range(line_content: &str, key: &str) -> Option<(usize, usize)> to the frontmatter module (crates/libaipm/src/frontmatter.rs)
The function takes a line like 'name: my-skill' and returns the 1-based (col, end_col) for the value portion after the colon
Handle whitespace between colon and value (e.g., 'name:  my-skill' should still work)
Handle values with trailing whitespace — trim_end before computing end_col
Return None if the key prefix is not found in the line
Write unit tests: exact match 'name: my-skill' returns (7, 15)
Write unit tests: extra spaces 'name:  my-skill' returns (8, 16)
Write unit tests: no match for wrong key returns None
Write unit tests: empty value after colon 'name: ' returns None or appropriate range
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings to verify no lint violations
Run cargo test --workspace to verify all tests pass

description: Populate end_line/end_col in skill/missing-name rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_missing_name.rs
When field is absent: set line to Frontmatter.end_line (closing ---), col to 1, end_line to same, end_col to 4
When field exists but is empty: use field_value_range() to compute col/end_col for the name field on its line
Update the Diagnostic construction to populate end_line and end_col with computed values
Add unit test: missing name field produces diagnostic with end_line = closing --- line, end_col = 4
Add unit test: empty name field produces diagnostic with correct col/end_col range
Run cargo test --workspace to verify no regressions

description: Populate end_line/end_col in skill/missing-description rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_missing_desc.rs
When field is absent: set line to Frontmatter.end_line (closing ---), col to 1, end_line to same, end_col to 4
When field exists but is empty: use field_value_range() to compute col/end_col for the description field
Update the Diagnostic construction to populate end_line and end_col
Add unit test: missing description field produces correct range pointing to closing delimiter
Add unit test: empty description field produces correct col/end_col range
Run cargo test --workspace to verify no regressions

description: Populate end_line/end_col in skill/name-too-long rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_too_long.rs
Use field_value_range() to compute col/end_col for the name field value on its frontmatter line
Set end_line equal to line (single-line frontmatter values)
Update the Diagnostic construction to populate all range fields
Add unit test: name exceeding 64 chars produces diagnostic with col pointing to value start and end_col pointing to value end
Run cargo test --workspace to verify no regressions

description: Populate end_line/end_col in skill/name-invalid-chars rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_name_invalid.rs
Use field_value_range() to compute col/end_col for the name field value
Set end_line equal to line (single-line value)
Update the Diagnostic construction
Add unit test: 'name: inv@lid!' produces diagnostic with col/end_col spanning 'inv@lid!'
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Populate end_line/end_col in skill/description-too-long rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_desc_too_long.rs
Use field_value_range() to compute col/end_col for the description field value
Set end_line equal to line
Update the Diagnostic construction
Add unit test: description exceeding 1024 chars produces diagnostic with correct range
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Populate end_line/end_col in skill/invalid-shell rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/skill_invalid_shell.rs
Use field_value_range() to compute col/end_col for the shell field value
Set end_line equal to line
Update the Diagnostic construction
Add unit test: 'shell: zsh' produces diagnostic with col/end_col spanning 'zsh'
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Populate end_line/end_col in agent/missing-tools rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/agent_missing_tools.rs
When tools field is absent: set line to Frontmatter.end_line (closing ---), col to 1, end_line to same, end_col to 4
When tools field exists but is empty: use field_value_range() for the tools field
Update the Diagnostic construction
Add unit test: missing tools field in agents/reviewer.md produces diagnostic at closing --- delimiter
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Populate end_col in plugin/broken-paths rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/broken_paths.rs
Identify the two diagnostic sites (around lines 79 and 134) where col is already set
Add end_col: Some(col_offset + pos + 1 + matched_ref.len()) where matched_ref is the ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/path string
Set end_line equal to line (same line)
Add unit test: broken path reference produces diagnostic with col and end_col spanning the full ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/path string
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Populate line/col/end_col in hook/unknown-event rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_unknown_event.rs
For each unknown event string, scan the JSON content line-by-line to find the line containing the event
Set line to the 1-based line number where the event string appears
Set col to the 1-based position of the event string on that line
Set end_col to col + event_string.len()
Set end_line equal to line
Add unit test: hooks.json with unknown event 'PreToolUse' produces diagnostic with line/col pointing to the event string
Verify existing tests still pass with the updated range information
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Populate col/end_col in hook/legacy-event-name rule (Phase 2 — G4)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/rules/hook_legacy_event.rs
The rule already computes line via content.lines().enumerate() scanning — extend to also find col position
Set col to the 1-based position of the legacy event string on the found line
Set end_col to col + legacy_event.len()
Set end_line equal to line
Add unit test: hooks.json with PascalCase event produces diagnostic with col/end_col spanning the event name
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Verify human reporter renders populated ranges correctly (Phase 2 verification)
steps: Read crates/libaipm/src/lint/reporter.rs around lines 179-190 where the human reporter computes byte-offset spans
Verify the existing logic handles the new case where both col and end_col are populated (not just col alone)
Run the existing reporter snapshot tests to confirm no regressions
Add a snapshot test with a diagnostic that has col=7, end_col=15 to verify the annotate-snippets underline matches the value span
Run cargo test --workspace

description: Add aipm lsp CLI subcommand with stdio transport (Phase 3 — G5)
steps: Add tower-lsp = '0.20' and tokio = { version = '1', features = ['io-std', 'macros', 'rt-multi-thread'] } to crates/aipm/Cargo.toml dependencies
Add a Commands::Lsp variant to the clap CLI definition in crates/aipm/src/main.rs
Create a new module crates/aipm/src/lsp.rs (or crates/aipm/src/lsp/mod.rs) for the LSP server implementation
Implement the tower_lsp::LanguageServer trait with initialize, initialized, shutdown, did_open, did_save, did_close handlers
In initialize response, advertise capabilities: textDocumentSync (openClose: true, change: Full, save: true), diagnosticProvider (interFileDependencies: true)
Implement workspace root detection: walk up from opened file to find directory containing aipm.toml
In did_open and did_save handlers: load lint config from aipm.toml, call libaipm::lint::lint(), group diagnostics by file_path
Implement to_lsp_range() conversion function: convert 1-based Diagnostic ranges to 0-based lsp_types::Range
Handle None line/col gracefully: line=None maps to Range(0,0)-(0,0) for file-level diagnostics
Map Diagnostic.severity to DiagnosticSeverity::Error or Warning
Map rule_id to diagnostic code (NumberOrString::String), help_url to code_description.href
Set diagnostic source to 'aipm'
Publish diagnostics via client.publish_diagnostics() for each file with findings
Publish empty diagnostics for files that previously had diagnostics but now have none (clears stale markers)
Add debounce logic (300ms delay) to avoid redundant lint passes on rapid saves
Wire the Lsp command in main.rs to start the server on stdio using tower_lsp::Server::new(stdin, stdout)
Verify cargo build --workspace compiles without errors
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings

description: LSP textDocument/completion for aipm.toml rule IDs and severity values (Phase 3 — G6)
steps: Implement the completion handler in the LSP server (tower_lsp::LanguageServer::completion method)
Build a static rule lookup table at server startup by instantiating all rules and collecting id(), name(), default_severity()
Detect cursor context: determine if the cursor is inside [workspace.lints] in aipm.toml
At key position (typing a new rule override): offer all 19 rule IDs as CompletionItems with detail showing the rule name and default severity
At value position (after = sign): offer severity strings 'allow', 'warn', 'error', 'deny', 'warning' as CompletionItems
After typing { for detailed object: offer 'level' and 'ignore' keys
Set CompletionItem.kind to CompletionItemKind::Property for rule IDs and CompletionItemKind::Value for severity strings
Add integration test: send textDocument/completion at key position, assert all 19 rule IDs appear
Add integration test: send textDocument/completion at value position, assert severity strings appear
Run cargo test --workspace

description: LSP textDocument/hover for rule ID documentation (Phase 3 — G7)
steps: Implement the hover handler in the LSP server (tower_lsp::LanguageServer::hover method)
Use the static rule lookup table (built for completions) to resolve rule ID strings to Rule trait data
When hovering over a quoted rule ID string in [workspace.lints], return markdown content: **rule_id** (default_severity) + help_text + help_url link
Return None for hover outside [workspace.lints] or over non-rule-ID text
Add integration test: send textDocument/hover over 'skill/missing-name', assert markdown contains rule name, Warning severity, and help URL
Add integration test: send textDocument/hover over a non-rule string, assert no hover
Run cargo test --workspace

description: LSP server integration tests for all 6 feature kinds (Phase 3 testing)
steps: Create test fixtures directory for LSP integration tests (crates/aipm/tests/lsp/ or similar)
Create fixture: SKILL.md with invalid name chars in skills/default/ dir
Create fixture: agents/reviewer.md missing tools frontmatter
Create fixture: hooks/hooks.json with unknown event 'InvalidEvent'
Create fixture: .ai/test-plugin/aipm.toml with broken ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/ reference
Create fixture: .ai/test-plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json missing version field
Create fixture: .ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json with nonexistent source path
Write integration test: spawn aipm lsp, send initialize, send textDocument/didOpen for each fixture, assert publishDiagnostics response contains expected rule_id, severity, and range
Write integration test for stale clearing: open file with errors, fix content, save, verify diagnostics clear
Write unit test for to_lsp_range(): line=1,col=1 maps to Position(0,0); line=None maps to Range(0,0)-(0,0); end_col=None falls back to end-of-line
Run cargo test --workspace

description: VS Code extension scaffold with package.json and tomlValidation (Phase 4 — G3)
steps: Create vscode-aipm/ directory at the repository root
Create vscode-aipm/package.json with: name vscode-aipm, displayName aipm — AI Package Manager, activationEvents [workspaceContains:**/aipm.toml]
Add extensionDependencies: [tamasfe.even-better-toml] to require Taplo
Add contributes.tomlValidation: [{ fileMatch: aipm.toml, url: ./schemas/aipm.toml.schema.json }]
Add contributes.configuration with aipm.lint.enable (boolean, default true) and aipm.path (string, default aipm)
Add dependencies: { vscode-languageclient: ^9.0.0 }
Copy schemas/aipm.toml.schema.json to vscode-aipm/schemas/aipm.toml.schema.json
Create vscode-aipm/tsconfig.json with standard VS Code extension TypeScript config
Create vscode-aipm/.vscodeignore to exclude source files from packaged extension
Run npm install in vscode-aipm/ to install dependencies
Verify package.json is valid by running vsce ls (or npx @vscode/vsce ls)

description: VS Code extension activation and LSP client connection (Phase 4 — G3)
steps: Create vscode-aipm/src/extension.ts
Import workspace, ExtensionContext from vscode and LanguageClient, LanguageClientOptions, ServerOptions, TransportKind from vscode-languageclient/node
In activate(): read aipm.lint.enable config — return early if disabled
Read aipm.path config (default: 'aipm') for the binary location
Define serverOptions: { command: aipmPath, args: ['lsp', '--stdio'], transport: TransportKind.stdio }

Define documentSelector with precise patterns for all 6 feature kinds: **/skills/SKILL.md, **/skills/*/SKILL.md (skills), **/agents/*.md (agents — NOT AGENT.md), **/hooks/hooks.json (hooks), **/.ai/*/aipm.toml (plugin manifests), **/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (pluginJson), **/.ai/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json (marketplace)
Create LanguageClient instance and call client.start()
In deactivate(): call client?.stop()
Handle LSP spawn failure: catch error from client.start(), show VS Code notification 'aipm binary not found — install aipm or set aipm.path in settings'
Add context.subscriptions.push(client) for proper cleanup
Build the extension: npx tsc -p vscode-aipm/tsconfig.json
Verify the compiled output exists at vscode-aipm/out/extension.js

description: LSP workspace detection for multi-root and nested projects (Phase 3 infrastructure)
steps: Implement workspace root detection in the LSP server: given a file URI, walk up parent directories until aipm.toml is found
Cache workspace roots to avoid repeated filesystem walks
Handle multi-root VS Code workspaces: each root with an aipm.toml gets its own lint context
Handle case where no aipm.toml exists: publish empty diagnostics, log trace-level message
Add unit test: file at /project/.ai/plugin/skills/default/SKILL.md finds root at /project/ containing aipm.toml
Add unit test: file outside any workspace with aipm.toml returns no root
Add unit test: nested project with own aipm.toml uses the nearest root, not a parent one
Run cargo test --workspace

description: LSP graceful error handling for missing binary and missing config (Phase 3/4)
steps: In the LSP server: handle libaipm::lint::lint() returning errors gracefully — log the error, publish empty diagnostics, do not crash
Handle aipm.toml parse errors: if [workspace.lints] section is malformed, log a warning and use default config
In the VS Code extension: detect if aipm lsp process exits unexpectedly and show a user notification
In the VS Code extension: detect if aipm binary is not found on PATH and show actionable notification with link to install docs
Add integration test: LSP server handles workspace with no aipm.toml without crashing
Add integration test: LSP server handles malformed [workspace.lints] section without crashing

description: LSP debounce and startup performance (Phase 3 — 7.1)
steps: Add debounce logic in the LSP server: buffer rapid textDocument/didSave notifications with a 300ms delay before triggering re-lint
Verify LSP server initialize response completes in <100ms by measuring in integration tests
Verify full lint pass on a typical fixture project (dozen features) completes in <500ms
Build the rule lookup table (for completions/hover) once at initialize, not on each request
Add tracing spans around lint execution to measure performance in production

description: JSON Schema positive/negative test fixtures for schema validation (Phase 1 testing)
steps: Create schemas/tests/ directory (or similar) for schema test fixtures

Create valid.toml: aipm.toml with [workspace.lints] containing 'skill/missing-name' = 'allow', 'hook/unknown-event' = { level = 'warn', ignore = ['legacy/**'] }, and [workspace.lints.ignore] paths = ['vendor/**']
Create valid-with-other-sections.toml: aipm.toml with [package], [workspace], [dependencies] sections alongside [workspace.lints] — must pass since additionalProperties: true
Create invalid-unknown-rule.toml: aipm.toml with [workspace.lints] containing 'skill/mising-name' (typo) — must fail since additionalProperties: false on lints
Create invalid-wrong-type.toml: aipm.toml with 'skill/missing-name' = 42 (number instead of string/object) — must fail
Write a test script or CI step that validates these fixtures against the schema

description: Range population unit tests for all modified rules (Phase 2 testing)
steps: For each modified rule (skill/missing-name, skill/missing-description, skill/name-too-long, skill/name-invalid-chars, skill/description-too-long, skill/invalid-shell, agent/missing-tools, plugin/broken-paths, hook/unknown-event, hook/legacy-event-name):
Write unit test asserting end_line and end_col are populated (not None) for diagnostics where a specific field/value is found
Write unit test asserting col is populated correctly (1-based column number)
Write unit test asserting end_line equals line for single-line values
Write unit test for the missing-field case: end_line/end_col point to closing --- delimiter (col=1, end_col=4)
Write unit test for broken_paths: end_col = col + matched_ref.len()
Write unit test for hook rules: col and end_col span the event string within the JSON content
Run cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings
Run cargo test --workspace
Run cargo +nightly llvm-cov to verify branch coverage >= 89%

description: LSP coordinate conversion unit tests (Phase 3 testing)
steps: Write unit test for to_lsp_range: Diagnostic with line=1, col=1, end_line=1, end_col=10 maps to Range { start: Position(0,0), end: Position(0,9) }
Write unit test: Diagnostic with line=None, col=None maps to Range { start: Position(0,0), end: Position(0,0) } (file-level)
Write unit test: Diagnostic with line=5, col=3, end_line=None, end_col=None maps to Range { start: Position(4,2), end: Position(4, u32::MAX-1) } (full-line fallback)
Write unit test: Diagnostic with line=1, col=None (no column) maps to Range { start: Position(0,0), end: Position(0, u32::MAX-1) }
Run cargo test --workspace
